_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the Lvl 2 'Leather Kit Complete' archer and produce the Lvl 3 'Full Leather Scout' variant (Tier 1 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-04): Full Leather Scout — first gen succeeded, all 5 adds landed but character scale drifted (90.8% vertical fill vs L1's 74.1%, headroom 5.9% vs 24.5%).
v2 (2026-05-04): same content + explicit FRAMING/SCALE LOCK block + multi-image input (image_urls[0]=L2 keeper for content, image_urls[1]=L1 for scale anchor).

v3 (2026-05-04): added precise pose description + switched scale-anchor input from Source/Ref/Archer/archer1.png to UnitProgression/Archer/Refs/L1_Base.png (rescaled/reframed by user — 1024x1024 native, supersedes original). Updated framing numbers in the FRAMING/SCALE block accordingly. Locked v1 keeper but later flagged for arm regression: the linen bandage came back on the right arm and the left bracer lost its cross-strap binding.

v4 (2026-05-04): ARM PRESERVATION fix. Lead the EDIT GOAL with explicit verification of both forearm bracers (ONE strap on bow arm, TWO straps on draw arm, NO bandage anywhere). Strong negative-list against bandage restoration and against rendering bracers as plain leather sleeves without cross-strap bindings.

audit (2026-05-05): stripped level-reference jargon (L1/L2/.../L10/L1_Base) from prompt body — replaced with 'input image'/'first input image'/'second input image' or descriptive phrasing. Pruned forward-looking negatives that referenced future levels. Kept all visible-feature descriptions intact.
audit (2026-05-05): removed 'transparent' from background description (model cannot emit alpha; produces baked-in checkerboard pattern). Background is now described only as 'clean white'.

v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow' from face descriptions. Reason: model was over-rendering the canonical faint stubble as a five o'clock shadow, especially at L8 where the half-visor frames the lower face. Stubble removed from positive prompt language; canonical face is now clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin with no facial hair. (Prompt body was reflowed to remove redundant separators after stubble removal.)

v5_detail_economy (2026-05-07): Dropped add (5) SECOND LEATHER HIP POUCH per AD FeedbackV0 belt-kit minimization (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23): dagger sheath is the ONLY belt accessory through the chain. Add (4) DAGGER SHEATH rewritten to standalone positioning at the anatomical-RIGHT hip (no longer 'tucked behind the existing pouch' — L2 v3-chain has no pouch). PRESERVE block: removed 'preserve existing leather hip pouch' clause (L2 keeper has no pouch). Negative block: 'exactly TWO pouches total + new knife sheath' → blanket 'DO NOT add ANY hip pouch / belt pouch / leather satchel / tool-loop ring; the dagger sheath is the only belt accessory'. visible_adds_count 5 → 4. Input updated to v3/L2 composite. Quilted gambeson texture on red panels (add 2) retained — surface texture, not stacked surface ornament; reads as structural reinforcement at thumbnail scale.

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=97.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L2/composite/archer_L2_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL ARM PRESERVATION — before applying any upgrade, FIRST verify and preserve the forearm state visible in the input image: BOTH FOREARMS are wrapped in matte warm-brown leather wrist bracers (NO bandage anywhere on either arm). The BOW arm (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT side of frame) has a leather wrist bracer with ONE visible cross-strap binding wrapping it, and the bare hand grips the bow at hip height. The DRAW arm (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT side of frame) has a matching leather wrist bracer with TWO visible cross-strap bindings (slightly more developed than the bow-arm bracer) and the bare hand is held forward in a tight closed fist at chest center. Both bracers must remain VISIBLE in the output with their cross-strap bindings clearly painted as horizontal bindings wrapping the forearm. There is NO white linen wrap on either arm or hand, only matte warm-brown leather bracers with cross-strap bindings. Do NOT render either bracer as a plain leather sleeve / vambrace / arm wrap without visible cross-strap bindings — the cross-strap binding is the bracer's defining detail. EDIT GOAL — Apply FOUR visible additive upgrades to the archer in the input image (the content reference). Do not apply any of All upgrades are in the warm-brown leather palette to match his existing belt, boots, bracers, and X-strap. The four upgrades together represent the 'Full Leather Scout' rank step — his leather kit is now fully built out and he reads as a seasoned scout. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT ===== (1) LEATHER PAULDRON CAPS ON BOTH SHOULDERS — add small soft-leather rounded caps over both deltoids, sitting on top of his bare biceps. Each pauldron cap is dome-shaped, about three inches across at the widest, made of slightly DARKER warm-brown leather than the bracers (so they read as their own piece, not as bracer extensions), with a single painted seam running over the top of each cap from front to back, soft warm highlights catching the dome curve, and a slightly darker shadow underneath where the cap meets the bare bicep. The biceps remain visible BELOW the pauldron caps — only the very top of the deltoid is covered. (2) QUILTED GAMBESON TUNIC TEXTURE — add visible quilted vertical fold lines down EACH RED PANEL of the tunic (the vertical red center panel running down the chest AND both red side panels along the flanks). Each red panel gets four to five evenly-spaced thin vertical quilted seams, painted with subtle darker fold lines and soft highlights, reading as a reinforced gambeson rather than thin cloth. The mustard-yellow base of the tunic stays SMOOTH (no quilting on the yellow), so the quilting reads as a deliberate reinforcement applied to the red panels only. (3) BROWN LEATHER GREAVES ON THE LOWER LEGS — add leather wrap-style greaves covering both lower legs from the top of each boot's folded ankle cuff up to JUST BELOW THE KNEE, sitting OVER the olive-green trousers (the trousers are still visible above the knee but covered from knee down). The greaves are matte warm-brown leather, with a single visible cross-strap binding wrapping each greave near the calf (matching the bracer style), painted with subtle leather grain and soft warm highlights. The boots themselves stay unchanged — chunky warm-brown leather with the darker folded ankle cuff. The greaves transition cleanly into the boot tops with no visible gap. (4) SMALL DARK-WOOD DAGGER IN A LEATHER SHEATH — add a small functional dagger in a brown leather sheath hanging from the belt at his anatomical-RIGHT hip (the viewer-LEFT side of the belt line). The sheath is the FIRST and ONLY accessory hanging from the belt — no pouches, no satchels, no tool-loop rings, no hanging tags accompany it. The sheath is matte warm-brown leather with a single painted seam down the side, about four inches long, hanging point-down from the belt with a clearly visible leather belt-loop attaching it. A short DARK-WOOD dagger handle protrudes UPWARD from the top of the sheath about one and a half inches, with a small painted brass capstone at the very top of the handle. The sheath sits cleanly against the hip with empty belt space on either side of it. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== Every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline his confident closed-mouth half-smirk; his thick dark brown eyebrows with the anatomical-LEFT brow (viewer-RIGHT side of the face) arched slightly higher than the other; his large warm brown eyes with a single bright clean catchlight in each iris; his small slightly upturned nose with the soft rosy painted tint on the tip; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with the soft rosy blush on the cheeks; his dark brown short hair visible at the temples and forehead under the hood; the deep crimson-red pointed cloth hood pulled fully up with the slight forward droop at the tip and the soft inner shadow framing the face; the matching crimson-red cape attached at both shoulders falling to about knee length with the soft painted vertical folds and the small inward curl on the viewer-LEFT side and the plain rolled hem; the sleeveless hip-length mustard-yellow tunic with the vertical red center panel and the red side panels (now with quilted vertical fold lines on the red panels per add (2) above, but the color blocking and silhouette of the tunic are unchanged); the existing brown leather wrist bracer on his anatomical-LEFT (bow) forearm with its single cross-strap binding; the existing brown leather wrist bracer on his anatomical-RIGHT (draw) forearm with its TWO cross-strap bindings; both hands BARE warm peachy-tan skin (no bandage, no glove); his bare anatomical-LEFT hand gripping the recurve short bow vertically at hip height; the recurve short bow with warm wood-brown limbs, dark-brown leather grip wrap, thin pale cream bowstring, recurve limb-tip profile — held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand; the existing brown leather X-strap chest harness running diagonally across the front of the chest from his anatomical-LEFT shoulder down to his anatomical-RIGHT hip with the small brass rivet at the central crossing — keep exactly as in the first input; the existing single wider warm-brown leather belt with the small brass rectangular buckle centered at the front and visible edge stitching — the belt is otherwise CLEAN with no hanging accessories before this edit (the new dagger sheath is the first); the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver slung diagonally across the back over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder with the strap visible where it crosses BEHIND, three to four arrows visible with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, on the viewer-RIGHT side of the frame) hangs DOWN at his side with the elbow only slightly bent, the bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height directly in front of the hip with the hand gripping the bow's central leather grip wrap — the bow-arm hand sits roughly at the level of the wide leather belt, NOT raised, NOT pulled across the body, NOT extended forward. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame) is bent sharply at the elbow at roughly NINETY DEGREES, with the FOREARM HELD HORIZONTALLY ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest / sternum level (parallel to the ground, NOT vertical, NOT angled down toward the bow, NOT extended forward away from the body). The bracered hand sits at the CENTER OF THE CHEST area, slightly toward the bow side, in a TIGHT CLOSED FIST shape (fingers curled into the palm, NOT an open hand, NOT loosely splayed, NOT reaching, NOT gripping anything visible). The closed fist is held with the KNUCKLES facing OUTWARD toward the viewer and the back of the hand visible; the fist does NOT touch the bow, does NOT touch the bowstring, does NOT touch the chest, and does NOT hold any object — it just hovers at chest center as a static ready-stance fist. The pose reads as a calm balanced HERO STANCE: bow arm down at hip on the viewer-right, bent draw arm horizontal across the chest with closed fist at center on the viewer-left. The pose is NOT an active draw, NOT a nocking motion, NOT a reach — both arms are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration, soft painted shading, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones, soft painted highlights on leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black, even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 75% of canvas vertical height (~765px tall) and approximately 57% of canvas horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of hood at ~13.5% from top of canvas (~138px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas (~120px below). Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the head or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background, no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT restore the white linen bandage on the draw arm or any arm or hand; DO NOT add ANY white linen wrap or cloth bandage anywhere; DO NOT render the bow-arm leather bracer as a plain leather sleeve without the single visible cross-strap binding; DO NOT render the draw-arm leather bracer as a plain leather sleeve without its TWO visible cross-strap bindings; DO NOT remove or hide the cross-strap bindings from either bracer; DO NOT change either bracer to a metal vambrace, gauntlet, glove, or scale piece — both stay matte warm-brown leather; DO NOT add a feather, plume, or any decoration to the hood; DO NOT add a leather forehead band or any structural addition to the hood; DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor sleeves on the arms; DO NOT make the pauldron caps metal, studded, or armored — they stay plain matte leather with a single seam only; DO NOT make the pauldrons large enough to cover the full bicep — they cap the deltoid only and the bicep stays visible below; DO NOT add a sword, axe, second knife, hammer, mace, or any weapon other than the bow, the back-quiver arrows, and the new small belt dagger; DO NOT make the belt dagger a sword or a long dagger — it is small, hand-length, with a dark-wood handle; DO NOT add ANY hip pouch, belt pouch, leather satchel, tool-loop ring, leather tag, or any other belt-mounted accessory — the dagger sheath is the ONLY accessory hanging from the belt at this level; DO NOT add a second X-strap, chest bandolier, or arrow-clip strap; DO NOT add a leather chest jerkin, leather over-vest, or leather chest piece; DO NOT add a breastplate, cuirass, or any plate armor; DO NOT add a helmet, skullcap, steel cap, or any metal headwear; DO NOT add a tabard, surcoat, or any waist garment over the tunic; DO NOT add tassets, arming-skirt strips, or any layered hip strips; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold buckle, gold limb tips on the bow, or any gold accents anywhere; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or engraved patterns; DO NOT make the greaves cover the knee or extend onto the thigh — they stop just below the knee; DO NOT make the greaves metal — they are matte warm-brown leather; DO NOT change the trouser color, the boot color, or the boot silhouette; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change any facial feature, hair, or skin tone; DO NOT change the hood color or shape; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow shape, color, or grip; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
